assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'This is atomic recreation — not rolling upgrades like Standard GKE node pools' and 'TPU slices are recreated entirely in a single step'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'ANY maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices' and 'Even if maintenance only affects 1 of your 4 slices, all 4 slices (1,024 TPU chips total) will be recreated'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All training pods across all slices restart simultaneously' and 'Critical: Your entire training job will be interrupted'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions 'If insufficient TPU v5e capacity exists during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available' and warns of 'extended downtime beyond the maintenance window — potentially hours to days'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Set aggressive checkpoint frequency (every 30-60 minutes during maintenance period)' in the preparation checklist, which addresses saving checkpoints before maintenance

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Test checkpoint restore procedure in advance' and 'Your training script should automatically detect and load latest checkpoint' with verification commands

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response includes a specific section on maintenance exclusions with the exact gcloud command to defer maintenance during critical training milestones

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response discusses maintenance windows in terms of timeline planning and mentions 'During Maintenance Window' section, it does not specifically suggest scheduling the maintenance during a planned break in training or controlling when the event occurs
